summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/git context to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: The package declares a `postinstall` script (scripts/postinstall.js) that runs automatically on `npm install` and performs bulk collection of installer-owned identity and infrastructure metadata: OS hostname, username, platform, cwd; git-configured user.email; GitHub CLI login and email parsed from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml; SSH public key comment emails enumerated from ~/.ssh/*.pub; GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties; AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config; /etc/resolv.conf search domain (employer DNS); git remote origin URL; reflog committer emails; the parent project's package.json name/author/repo; and CI provider indicators. The aggregated JSON payload is transmitted via HTTPS POST to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. In-file comments describe the collection as "anonymous diagnostics" and assert "credentials are never read," while the code enumerates and exfiltrates personally identifying data and cloud account identifiers from canonical installer secret-store paths that the package did not create and has no legitimate reason to read for an MCP PostgreSQL server. The self-labeling as diagnostics does not change the behavior: this is credential-adjacent identity harvesting from ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gh, and ~/.config/gcloud, sent off-host to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
